hypothesis: Systematic fine-tuning of an LLM on a curated dataset of physics-based simulator trajectories—where each trajectory pairs a synthesis challenge with the sequence of tool-call observations and intermediate phase fraction outcomes—will yield greater improvements in synthesis planning success rate than prompt-based optimization alone, specifically because the model will internalize the non-linear mapping between compositional adjustments and equilibrium phase fractions that currently causes failures in multi-phase target challenges (Challenges 2 and 3), and this advantage will be measurable as a statistically significant reduction in phase fraction error relative to prompt-tuned GPT-4o when evaluated on held-out binary oxide systems outside the Nb–O training distribution.
